seoTitle: Mitchelton vs Moa Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mitchelton and Moa Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mitchelton vs Moa Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mitchelton and Moa Island.
heading: Mitchelton vs Moa Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mitchelton, Queensland and Moa Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mitchelton has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Moa Island (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mitchelton or Moa Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mitchelton has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Moa Island scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mitchelton scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Moa Island scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mitchelton or Moa Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mitchelton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
